Do not rely on preprocess alone for who is being accused, because the target identity is not decisively grounded there. Replace any unsupported assumption about which character the line refers to with the new evidence from the bounded ASR and accusation-moment frames. The answer-critical field is the visual identity of the accused person, mapped to the option descriptions. The modality that matters most is the combination of accusation dialogue timing and the corresponding frames showing who the man in the light gray jacket is indicating/facing. If the new evidence still does not clearly distinguish among the listed appearances, leave the answer unresolved rather than guessing.
